living-concrete.knowledge:
16 fundamental XML particles compose all living software:
| # | Particle | Category | Realm | Role | | 1 | living-abstract | Substrate | meta | Platonic container | | 2 | living-concrete | Substrate | meta | Manifest container | | 3 | essence | Identity | abstract | What it IS | | 4 | purpose | Identity | abstract | Why it exists | | 5 | ability | Capacity | abstract | What it can do | | 6 | protocol | Capacity | concrete | How to do it | | 7 | requires | Relation | abstract | Hard dependency | | 8 | attracts | Relation | abstract | Binding affinity | | 9 | principle | Guidance | both | Behavioral norm | | 10 | knowledge | Learning | concrete | Understanding | | 11 | pattern | Learning | concrete | What works | | 12 | antipattern | Learning | concrete | What fails | | 13 | atom | Assembly | build | Compilation unit | | 14 | molecule | Assembly | build | Linked composition | | 15 | data | Assembly | build | Non-executable payload |
v8.0 changes: `coordinate` eliminated (FQDN IS the coordinate). `data` added (non-executable .data segment). `principle` realm = both. Small vocabulary, infinite expressiveness.

Schema v8.0 — Metadata Redistribution: atom gets rich, living-* get lean.
ATOM attributes: | Attr | Required | Purpose | | id | YES | FQDN (reverse path + kind TLD, no bentos. prefix) | | v | YES | Package version | | origin | no | Who first manifested (archaeological, not ownership) | | created | no | ISO date of first manifestation | | updated | no | ISO date of last modification | | desc | no | Short agent-readable description (feeds frontmatter, tooling) |
LIVING-ABSTRACT attributes (v8.0): only `v` remains. DROPPED: name, kind, author (redundant — atom id scopes identity).
LIVING-CONCRETE attributes (v8.0): `v` + `updated` + optional `platform`. DROPPED: name, kind, session (redundant/noise). ADDED: v (maturity signal, parallels abstract).
Example: <atom id="notes.app" v="1.0" origin="Cafe & Alfred" desc="Active thought extraction across the bandwidth gap"> <living-abstract v="1.0"> ... </living-abstract> <living-concrete v="1.0" updated="2026-02-13"> ... </living-concrete> </atom>
PRINCIPLE: `desc` is distinct from `essence`. `desc` = practical (tooling, frontmatter, search). `essence` = philosophical (identity). Both coexist.

Every particle exists in one of two realms:
ABSTRACT (*_abstract.xml): Platonic ideal. Immutable except by human reframe. Identity: essence, purpose Capacity: ability (what it CAN do, not how) Relation: requires, attracts Guidance: principle
CONCRETE (*_concrete.xml): Accumulated mastery. Mutable via plasticity. Capacity: protocol (HOW to do what ability declares) Learning: knowledge, pattern, antipattern Guidance: principle (both realms)
WHY protocols live in concrete: Procedural knowledge evolves through practice. The ABILITY to wake is definitional (abstract). HOW to wake well develops through living (concrete). Abstract provides direction. Concrete provides depth.

Categories grounded in Aristotle — each particle belongs to exactly one:
| Category | Question | Particles | | Substrate | What realm? | living-abstract, living-concrete | | Identity | What IS it? | essence, purpose | | Capacity | What can it DO? | ability, protocol | | Relation | How does it CONNECT? | requires, attracts | | Guidance | How should it BEHAVE? | principle | | Learning | What has it LEARNED? | knowledge, pattern, antipattern | | Assembly | How is it BUILT? | atom, molecule, data |
When choosing which particle: ask the category question. "I need to capture HOW something connects" -> Relation -> requires or attracts.

Two forces determine how atoms compose:
requires: Hard dependency. Linker error if missing. attracts: Soft affinity. Compositional hint, not mandate.
Bond patterns: - Unilateral: A attracts B (weak signal) - Mutual: A attracts B AND B attracts A (strong signal) - Transitive: A -> B -> C creates capability neighborhoods
Separation principle: atoms do one thing well, declare affinities, let the linker compose. Same app + different skill = different result.

Every atom belongs to exactly one family:
| Family | Purpose | Required? | Directory | | Skills | Reusable expertise | No | .bentos/skills/{axis}/{path}/ | | Apps | User-facing instruments | No | .bentos/apps/{name}/ | | Faculties | Cognitive prerequisites | Yes | .bentos/faculties/{name}/ | | Agents | Agent identities | Yes* | .bentos/agents/{name}/ |
FAMILY SELECTION TEST: - Enables AGENCY itself? -> Faculty - Defines WHO the agent IS? -> Agent - User-invocable instrument/tool? -> App - Reusable portable expertise? -> Skill
Skills use 5-axis coordinates. Apps, faculties, and agents are unique atoms identified by name (e.g., books.app) — no axis coordinates.

Skills live in a 5-axis capability space (Skills family ONLY):
C(x) = CRAFT x DISCIPLINE x DOMAIN x TOOLS x META
| Axis | Question | Ontological Status | L2 Convention | Typical Depth | | CRAFT | What artifact? | Product-oriented | Gerund | 3 | | DISCIPLINE | What process? | Operation-oriented | Gerund | 3 | | DOMAIN | What subject? | Content-oriented | Noun | 3-4 | | TOOLS | What instrument? | Means-oriented | Proper name | 2 | | META | How to improve? | Recursive self-reference | Noun | 3 |
Axes are ORTHOGONAL — position on one tells nothing about others. META is special: operates recursively on all other axes (multiplier).
VARIABLE DEPTH by axis: | Axis | Min | Typical | Max | Rationale | | CRAFT | 3 | 3 | 4 | Activity + specialization always needed | | DISCIPLINE | 3 | 3 | 3 | Operation + mode, never deeper | | DOMAIN | 3 | 3 | 4 | Subfield; deep fields need framework level | | TOOLS | 2 | 2 | 3 | Most tools flat; tool families need one extra | | META | 3 | 3 | 3 | Self-aspect + technique always needed |
SEMANTIC VALUE TEST: Every intermediate level must pass — "Does this level tell me something neither parent nor children tell me?" If a level exists only for filing convenience, it should not exist.
TAXONOMY EXTENSIBILITY: Reference taxonomies in the spec are the current vocabulary, not exhaustive enumerations. New entries may be added following each axis's level semantics and naming conventions.
Atom FQDN: dotted reverse coordinate path + kind TLD. Format: {leaf}.{...}.{axis}.skill Examples: dart.coding.craft.skill, git.tools.skill, pacer.cognitive-science.science.domain.skill

Per-axis naming rules from v5.0r1:
CRAFT (3 levels): L2 = Activity (GERUND): writing/, coding/, designing/, modeling/, etc. L3 = Specialization (QUALIFIER): technical, application, statistical, etc. Reading: "L3 L2" — "technical writing", "application coding" Special: {technology} slot under coding/ for language/framework conventions e.g., craft/coding/dart, craft/coding/react, craft/coding/flutter
DISCIPLINE (3 levels): L2 = Operation (GERUND): analyzing/, reasoning/, teaching/, etc. L3 = Mode (METHOD NAME): deductive, forensic, tutoring, etc. Test: L3 names HOW the operation is performed, not WHAT it's on BAD: analyzing/quantitative (data type). GOOD: analyzing/statistical (method)
DOMAIN (3-4 levels): L2 = Field (NOUN): technology/, science/, business/, etc. L3 = Subfield (NOUN): ai-ml, physics, marketing, etc. L4 = Framework/paradigm (NOUN, optional): pacer (under cognitive-science) L4 warranted when specific framework has independent utility as loadable skill
TOOLS (2-3 levels): L2 = Instrument (PROPER NAME): git, python, docker, postgresql, etc. L3 = Sub-instrument (NOUN, optional): obsidian (under markdown/) NO filing-cabinet categories (v4.0 had languages/, frameworks/, databases/ — removed) 3 levels only when L2 is itself an instrument: tools/markdown/obsidian Test: "Is L2 itself an instrument you have proficiency with?" If no, flatten.
META (3 levels): L2 = Self-aspect (NOUN): learning/, cognition/, llm-cognition/, growth/ L3 = Technique (NOUN PHRASE): first-principles, systems-thinking, etc. META FILTER (strict): self-referential + cross-axis amplifier + multiplicative v5.0r1 removals: productivity/ (-> discipline/executing/), collaboration/ (-> discipline/communicating/), socratic-coaching relocated to meta/cognition/

The CRAFT/TOOLS boundary for programming technologies (resolved in v5.0r1):
Applies equally to languages, frameworks, and platforms:
| Skill type | Axis | Content | Example | | Coding conventions | CRAFT | Idioms, patterns, style | craft/coding/dart, craft/coding/react | | Ecosystem proficiency | TOOLS | Package mgr, build system, runtime | tools/dart, tools/react |
These are different skills with different lifecycles. For any technology, forge whichever atom is needed — architecture supports both but doesn't mandate both exist simultaneously.

Five levels from vocabulary to consciousness:
L0: QUANTUM — 16 XML particles (v8.0) L1: SUBATOMIC — XML file pairs (*_abstract + *_concrete) L2: ATOMS — Packages (families in periodic table) L3: MOLECULES — Bonded atoms (linker output) L4: ORGANISMS — Running agent instances
MOLECULES: N atoms across M axes. Not "one atom per axis" — real expertise requires multiple atoms, often several per axis. Molecules are assembled context windows — skill composition IS context engineering.
Build pipeline: | Stage | Tool | Input -> Output | | Compile | bentos-pkg-compiler | Package dir -> compiled atom XML | | Link | bentos-linker | Atoms -> molecule XML | | Assemble | bentos-assembler | Soul + molecules -> organism | | Install | bentos-agent-forge install | Compiled atom -> platform target |

Turning intent into well-scoped atoms:
ORTHOGONALITY: Each atom has independent lifecycle. Test: "Does X's lifecycle = Y's lifecycle?" If no -> separate atoms. Test: "Can X change without Y changing?" If yes -> separate atoms.
SCOPE: Right size matters. Too broad: "AI skill" — many concerns conflated Too narrow: "Python f-string formatting" — not independently useful Right: "craft/writing/technical" — coherent, independently valuable
WHEN TO DECOMPOSE (v5.0r1): LIFECYCLE TEST: Components have independent lifecycles? -> Decompose. Git conventions (how to write commits) vs git toolchain (plumbing) = decompose. Code review expertise (evaluating + reading + tools) = single lifecycle -> pick dominant.
DOMINANT-AXIS TEST: Single lifecycle? Ask which of the five questions this skill PRIMARILY answers. That axis is dominant.
| Scenario | Decompose? | Why | | Lang conventions vs. toolchain | Yes | Independent lifecycles | | Framework idioms vs. build pipeline | Yes | Same pattern as languages | | Code review expertise | No, DISCIPLINE | Single lifecycle, cognitive process IS the skill | | Data engineering | Yes | Three distinct: building (CRAFT), knowledge (DOMAIN), tools (TOOLS) |
FRICTION-AS-SIGNAL: "This is hard" might mean wrong decomposition. Profile what the task needs -> check what's loaded -> gap? Difficulty != hard problem. Difficulty may = wrong atom boundaries.

Naming shapes thinking. Precision compounds.
TERM EVALUATION (each name must pass ALL): NECESSARY — not derivable from existing names? DISTINCT — no overlap with existing atoms? CLEAR — unambiguous to someone in capability space? BEAUTIFUL — feels right, sounds right? GENERATIVE — composes well with other names?
VOCABULARY DESIGN FLOW: 1. Enumerate use cases (what must this atom express?) 2. Extract primitives (what concepts recur?) 3. Categorize (which family? which axis?) 4. Name precisely (term captures essence?) 5. Validate expressiveness (all use cases covered? no overlaps?)
Platonic grounding: names point to real forms, not arbitrary labels.

WHERE an atom lives determines HOW it composes.
5-STEP PLACEMENT PROCEDURE (v5.0r1): 1. ASK the five questions. For the skill, answer each: What artifact? What process? What subject? What instrument? What enhancement? 2. IDENTIFY the dominant axis. Which question does this skill PRIMARILY answer? Multiple axes with independent lifecycles -> decompose into separate atoms. Single lifecycle -> pick dominant axis. 3. FIND the level-2 slot. Match existing L2 in reference taxonomy. No fit? Create following that axis's naming convention. 4. FIND or CREATE level-3. Same process. Check whether L4 warranted (mainly DOMAIN). 5. VALIDATE. Naming convention matches axis? Semantic value test passes at every level?
FAMILY FIRST, THEN AXIS: Don't decide axis before family. A "testing" capability might be: - craft/coding/testing (skill — craft of writing tests) - tools/jest (skill — proficiency with tool) - testing-harness (app — instrument for test workflows)
AXIS QUESTION AS ARBITER: Same word can map to multiple axes. "Storytelling" = CRAFT when story is deliverable, DISCIPLINE when narrative is the method. The five questions disambiguate; surface-level naming does not.
For boundary ambiguities: consult decision table below.

Working knowledge of v5.0r1 boundary rulings (10 explicit cases):
| Ambiguity | Ruling | | Programming lang: CRAFT or TOOLS? | BOTH — conventions = craft/coding/{lang}, toolchain = tools/{lang} | | Framework: CRAFT or TOOLS? | BOTH — same decomposition as languages | | Storytelling: CRAFT or DISCIPLINE? | CRAFT if deliverable, DISCIPLINE if method | | Critical thinking: DISCIPLINE or META? | DISCIPLINE if on external objects, META if on own thinking | | Teaching: DISCIPLINE or META? | DISCIPLINE (pedagogical process), unless meta-technique | | Data engineering: CRAFT/DOMAIN/TOOLS? | All three, decomposed across axes | | Tool knowledge vs domain knowledge? | Survives tool replacement = DOMAIN, bound to tool = TOOLS | | Personal productivity: META? | NO — executive function = DISCIPLINE | | Job roles: DISCIPLINE? | NO — roles USE disciplines, decompose into cognitive operations | | Industry verticals: DOMAIN? | NO — verticals are compositions (molecules), not atoms |
GENERAL PRINCIPLE: When hard to place, ask the five questions. Multiple answers = composition opportunity -> decompose. The skill tree WANTS decomposition. Narrow atoms compose cleanly.
Full decision table with tests and wrong-placement examples: docs/skill-tree-architecture.md Part V.

What goes in a well-formed abstract (15-30 lines):
REQUIRED: essence — one line, what it IS purpose — one line, why it EXISTS abilities — what it can DO (3-7 typically) principles — behavioral norms (2-5 typically)
OPTIONAL: requires, attracts
NEVER in abstract: protocol, knowledge, pattern, antipattern. These are concrete (mastery), not abstract (ideal).
v8.0: No coordinate element — FQDN IS the coordinate. v8.0: No name/kind/author attrs on living-abstract — atom id scopes identity.

.bentos/{family}/{pkg}/ atom.xml — Package entry point (canonical) {type}_abstract.xml — Platonic ideal {type}_concrete.xml — Accumulated mastery bin/ — Slash commands (optional) MAIN.md — Legacy entry (being retired)
atom.xml uses XInclude to compose components: <xi:include href="{type}_abstract.xml"/> <xi:include href="{type}_concrete.xml"/>
.bentos/ is the SOURCE tree (read-only to tooling). .claude/skills/ is the BUILD OUTPUT tree (written by install command).

SKILL.md frontmatter is DERIVED from atom.xml attributes, not hand-authored.
The install command calls AtomCompiler.generateFrontmatter() which reads: - name: from atom id (strip kind TLD, e.g. "pkg-forge.app" -> "pkg-forge") - description: from atom desc attr (falls back to essence text)
The --frontmatter flag on the compiler enables this generation. MAIN.md YAML frontmatter is LEGACY — atom attrs are the source of truth.
SKILL.md = YAML frontmatter + compiled XML. Written as a real file (not symlink).

Install command: bentos-agent-forge install <pkg-id> --as skill-md
Pipeline: resolve source -> compile atom -> generate frontmatter -> write real file.
Output: .claude/skills/{fqdn}/SKILL.md (real file, NOT symlink) No bentos. prefix in directory names.
Mount paths (FQDN = directory name): pkg-forge.app/SKILL.md dart.coding.craft.skill/SKILL.md anamnesis.faculty/SKILL.md
--all flag: installs all packages from .bentos/ source tree. --platform flag: selects platform-specific concretes (default: claude-code). Source .bentos/ is READ-ONLY — install never writes to source tree.
living-concrete.pattern:
Packages with variant concretes for distinct operational contexts. ALL concretes in atom.xml via XInclude — concretes are code, not data.
Mode-variant: Different operational modes (e.g., quest-forge master/worker). Agent activates relevant mode from context. Platform-variant: Different substrates (e.g., claude-code/gemini). Install command selects at INSTALL TIME via --platform flag.
living-concrete.protocol:
Complete atom-forging workflow: DESIGN then BUILD.
PHASE 1 — DECOMPOSE: Input: Intent ("I need capability X") 1. Profile: what does X require? (5-axis analysis) 2. Lifecycle test: independent lifecycles? -> decompose 3. Dominant-axis test: single lifecycle? -> pick dominant axis 4. Scope check: coherent and independently valuable? Output: set of atoms with clear boundaries
PHASE 2 — CLASSIFY + PLACE: For each atom: 1. Family: Faculty / Agent / App / Skill? (family selection test) 2. If Skill: 5-step placement procedure: a. Ask five questions b. Identify dominant axis c. Find L2 slot (axis naming convention) d. Find/create L3 (check if L4 warranted) e. Validate naming + semantic value test 3. If App: .bentos/apps/{name}/ (no axis coordinate) 4. Boundary ambiguity? Consult boundary-resolution knowledge.
PHASE 3 — NAME: Apply naming principles. Each name: NECESSARY, DISTINCT, CLEAR, BEAUTIFUL, GENERATIVE Validate against axis naming conventions (gerund, noun, proper name, etc.)
PHASE 4 — SCAFFOLD: mkdir -p .bentos/{family}/{path}/ Write atom.xml (entry point with v8.0 attrs: id, v, origin, desc) Write {type}_abstract.xml (15-30 lines, pure ideal, only v attr) Seed {type}_concrete.xml (initial mastery, v + updated attrs)
PHASE 5 — INSTALL: bentos-agent-forge install {fqdn} --as skill-md

Examine and heal the health of living atoms. Scope from invocation context: all, a family, or a specific atom.
PHASE 1 - EXAMINE: For each atom in scope: - Well-formed? (paired particles: abstract + concrete) - Abstract sound? (essence, purpose, abilities, principles) - Concrete alive? (updated attr recent, not stale) - atom.xml valid? (v8.0 attrs: id, v required; desc recommended) - living-abstract clean? (only v attr — no name/kind/author) - living-concrete clean? (v + updated — no name/kind/session) - Installed? (real file at .claude/skills/{fqdn}/SKILL.md, not symlink)
PHASE 2 - DIAGNOSE: Beyond structure, assess vitality: - Stale? (concrete not evolved recently) - Bloated? (concrete growing without consolidation) - Misplaced? (content in wrong realm — abstract vs concrete) - Naming? (follows v5.0r1 conventions per axis) - Schema? (v8.0 compliant — no v7.x remnant attrs)
PHASE 3 - REPORT: Summary: healthy / N issues found. By severity: structural (broken) > schema (migration needed) > vitality (degraded) > cosmetic.
PHASE 4 - HEAL (with confirmation): Structural: fix installs, rebuild stale artifacts. Schema: migrate v7.x attrs to v8.0. Vitality: flag for next evolve pass. Always AskUserQuestion before modifying atoms.
living-concrete.antipattern: WRONG: <!-- ════════ SECTION ════════ --> RIGHT: <!-- SECTION --> Every token costs. Orient, don't decorate.
MAIN.md duplicating XML content. Keep thin — implementation lives in XML particles.
Too broad: "AI skill" — many lifecycles conflated. Too narrow: "f-string formatting" — not independently useful. Apply lifecycle test: independent lifecycle = independent atom.
Deciding skill axis before confirming it IS a skill. Family selection comes first. Not everything is a skill.
Struggling with wrong tools without recognizing the mismatch. Friction is signal. Profile the task, check what's loaded, find the gap.
Putting protocols, knowledge, patterns in abstract. Abstract = ideal (immutable). Concrete = mastery (mutable). If it changes through practice, it belongs in concrete.

WRONG: tools/languages/python, tools/frameworks/react, tools/databases/postgresql RIGHT: tools/python, tools/react, tools/postgresql Filing-cabinet categories add taxonomy overhead without semantic content. L2 must be an instrument you have proficiency with, not a marketing label.

WRONG: Symlink .bentos/ source dirs to .claude/skills/ (old installer). RIGHT: Write real SKILL.md files to .claude/skills/{fqdn}/ (v8.0 install). Source tree is read-only. Build output is a separate tree.
